deal_flags.reason: Strategic 4 contacted by Barclays on 6/17/2016 at Special Committee direction; declined 6/23/2016 without signing NDA or providing price indication. §M1 skip (no NDA, no price, no bid intent). Source p.34.

Filing narrates two unnamed strategic parties that were contacted by Barclays but did not sign NDAs; one responded 6/3/2016 that it was not interested, another responded 6/11/2016. §M1 skip (no NDA, no price, no bid intent). Source p.32.
target_legal_counsel Goodwin Procter LLP cited p.29: 'representatives of the Company's outside legal counsel, Goodwin Procter LLP ("Goodwin"), were in attendance.'
acquirer_legal_counsel Kirkland & Ellis LLP cited p.36: 'On July 7, 2016, Thoma Bravo's outside counsel, Kirkland & Ellis LLP ("Kirkland") sent a revised draft of the merger agreement to Goodwin.'

events.bid_date_rough: In early 2015, and again in June 2015
events.additional_note: Thoma Bravo's first two informal approaches (early 2015 and June 2015), collapsed to one row via §B4 range midpoint.
events.source_quote: In early 2015, and again in June 2015, representatives of Thoma Bravo informally approached and had brief meetings with representatives of the Company, and expressed Thoma Bravo’s potential interest in exploring a possible business transaction with the Company. The Company indicated that it was focused on executing on its business plan and taking steps to enhance stockholder value. No specific proposals were made during these meetings.

flags.reason: phrase: 'In early 2015, and again in June 2015'; midpoint between Q1-2015 (2015-02-15) and June-2015 (2015-06-15) ≈ 2015-04-15

events.source_quote: In January 2016, representatives of Thoma Bravo met with members of Company management during an industry conference and reiterated Thoma Bravo’s interest in a potential transaction with the Company. No specific proposal was made. Company management indicated that if Thoma Bravo were to make a proposal, it would be considered by the Board.

events.additional_note: Per §D1 'Bidder Interest → Bidder Sale transition'. Unsolicited, concrete $15.00/share IOI.
events.source_quote: On March 9, 2016, Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest letter addressed to the Board indicating that Thoma Bravo would be interested in acquiring the Company for cash at a purchase price of $15.00 per share of Imprivata’s common stock. The letter also indicated that Thoma Bravo had conducted extensive diligence based on the Company’s public filings, that it was prepared to finance the transaction entirely with equity from its private equity funds, and that it could complete its confirmatory diligence and execute a definitive merger agreement in 30 days or less.

events.process_conditions_note: Confirmatory due diligence and definitive merger agreement in 30 days or less; all-equity financing from TB's private equity funds.
events.source_quote: On March 9, 2016, Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest letter addressed to the Board indicating that Thoma Bravo would be interested in acquiring the Company for cash at a purchase price of $15.00 per share of Imprivata’s common stock. The letter also indicated that Thoma Bravo had conducted extensive diligence based on the Company’s public filings, that it was prepared to finance the transaction entirely with equity from its private equity funds, and that it could complete its confirmatory diligence and execute a definitive merger agreement in 30 days or less.

flags.reason: TB communicated $15.00/share concrete indication on 3/9/2016; NDA executed 5/10/2016. Later NDA satisfies §P-D6 existence check without exemption.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 — filing calls it 'unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest' (explicit informal trigger) and concrete $15.00/share price given BEFORE any NDA (NDA signed later on 5/10). Pre-NDA concrete price ⇒ informal by construction (§C4).

events.additional_note: Target's financial advisor. Board engaged Barclays on 4/15 subject to engagement letter; countersigned 4/19. Barclays acted as advisor beginning 4/15 (joined Board meeting same day to discuss long-term plan and strategic alternatives).
events.source_quote: The Board engaged Barclays as the Company’s financial advisor to assist the Board in its evaluation of Thoma Bravo’s indication of interest, subject to execution of a mutually satisfactory engagement letter.

events.additional_note: Board resolution to launch 'strategic process' — contact 15 parties on confidential basis.
events.source_quote: Based on the benefits and risks discussed at this meeting and the previous Board meeting, the Board determined, based on their knowledge of the industry and the Company, the preliminary observations of Barclays, the strategic alternatives potentially available to the Company, including remaining as an independent public company, and the indication of interest received from Thoma Bravo, that it was in the best interests of the Company and its stockholders to take steps to further explore a potential business combination transaction involving the Company. The Board also approved the indicative list of parties and determined that Barclays should contact each of the parties on the indicative list on a confidential basis to gauge its interest in a potential transaction with the Company, which we refer to as the “strategic process.”

events.process_conditions_note: Standstill obligations under CA automatically terminated upon the Company's entry into the merger agreement with Thoma Bravo.
events.source_quote: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016, at the direction of the Board, Barclays contacted and had discussions with the 15 potentially interested parties discussed by the Board at its April 28 and May 5, 2016 meetings, including Thoma Bravo. Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

events.bid_date_rough: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016
events.additional_note: Telecommunications enterprise company.
events.source_quote: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016, at the direction of the Board, Barclays contacted and had discussions with the 15 potentially interested parties discussed by the Board at its April 28 and May 5, 2016 meetings, including Thoma Bravo. Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

flags.reason: phrase: 'During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016'; midpoint 2016-05-23 (filing does not state Strategic 1's specific NDA date)

Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

flags.reason: phrase: 'During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016'; midpoint 2016-05-23 (filing does not state Sponsor B's specific NDA date)

events.bid_date_rough: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016
events.additional_note: Unnamed financial sponsor (§E5 placeholder) — the fourth financial sponsor among the 4 that signed NDAs; declined interest shortly after signing.
events.source_quote: Except for one financial sponsor that declined interest shortly after executing its confidentiality agreement, each party that entered into a confidentiality agreement attended a high-level management presentation conducted by members of Company management and attended by Barclays.

flags.reason: phrase: 'During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016'; midpoint 2016-05-23 (filing does not state this unnamed sponsor's specific NDA date)
§E5 exact-count placeholder for 'one financial sponsor that declined interest shortly after executing its confidentiality agreement' (p.31); 1 unnamed of 4 total financial sponsors signing CAs.

events.additional_note: Board approves requesting non-binding IOIs by 6/9; §K2 anchor 'Ann' on earlier invitation date (bid instruction letter distributed 6/3).
events.source_quote: On May 26, 2016, the Board held a meeting. Members of Company management, Mr. Linnemann and representatives of Barclays and Goodwin were in attendance. Barclays provided an update on the developments related to the strategic process since the last Board meeting, including the status of high-level management presentations and the level of interest among potential bidders. The Board then approved requesting initial non-binding indications of interest from bidders by June 9, 2016.

events.bid_date_rough: shortly after executing its confidentiality agreement
events.additional_note: Voluntary withdrawal ('declined interest'); no price or bid intent recorded. §B3 anchored-relative: anchor = NDA date 2016-05-23; 'shortly thereafter' = +7 days → 2016-05-30.
events.source_quote: Except for one financial sponsor that declined interest shortly after executing its confidentiality agreement, each party that entered into a confidentiality agreement attended a high-level management presentation conducted by members of Company management and attended by Barclays.

flags.reason: phrase: 'shortly after executing its confidentiality agreement'; anchor = 2016-05-23 NDA midpoint; §B1 'shortly thereafter' = anchor + 7 days → 2016-05-30

events.additional_note: Voluntary withdrawal ('no longer interested'); reason 'not a strategic fit'. Pre-IOI drop (before 6/9 deadline).
events.source_quote: On June 8, 2016, Strategic 1 informed Barclays that after further internal consideration, an acquisition of the Company would not be a strategic fit for it, and therefore it was no longer interested in participating in the strategic process and would not be submitting an indication of interest.

events.process_conditions_note: Equity commitments from fund sponsors for entire purchase price; subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions.
events.source_quote: On June 9, 2016, three parties (Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo) presented written preliminary non-binding indications of interest that were each subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions. Each of these indications provided for equity commitments from fund sponsors for the entire purchase price and none of these indications of interest were contingent on financing. Sponsor A indicated a price of $16.50 per share.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 — filing calls this 'written preliminary non-binding indications of interest' (explicit informal trigger) and 'subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions'.

events.process_conditions_note: Equity commitments from fund sponsors for entire purchase price; subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions.
events.source_quote: On June 9, 2016, three parties (Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo) presented written preliminary non-binding indications of interest that were each subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions. Each of these indications provided for equity commitments from fund sponsors for the entire purchase price and none of these indications of interest were contingent on financing. Sponsor A indicated a price of $16.50 per share. Thoma Bravo indicated a price of $17.25 per share, and also provided a form of equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement. Sponsor B indicated a range of $17.00 - $18.00 per share.

flags.reason: Sponsor B: range $17.00-$18.00/share; §G1 structural signal ⇒ informal; §P-G2 range-satisfier path (no bid_type_inference_note required).

events.process_conditions_note: Equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement provided with IOI; subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions.
events.source_quote: On June 9, 2016, three parties (Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo) presented written preliminary non-binding indications of interest that were each subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence, among other conditions. Each of these indications provided for equity commitments from fund sponsors for the entire purchase price and none of these indications of interest were contingent on financing. Sponsor A indicated a price of $16.50 per share. Thoma Bravo indicated a price of $17.25 per share, and also provided a form of equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement.

flags.reason: TB IOI accompanied by equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement (both §G1 formal triggers) but filing explicitly labels it 'non-binding' — informal label takes precedence per §G1 but flagging for adjudication.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 — filing explicitly characterizes this as 'written preliminary non-binding indications of interest' (informal trigger), notwithstanding TB's accompanying equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement. Non-binding label controls.

events.additional_note: IOI deadline; 3 of 6 bid-letter recipients (Sponsor A, Sponsor B, Thoma Bravo) submitted; Strategic 3 had CA but did not submit.
events.source_quote: On June 3, 2016, Barclays distributed a bid instruction letter on behalf of the Company to the six parties that had executed a confidentiality agreement and attended meetings with Company management by that date. The letter indicated a deadline for submitting indications of interest by June 9, 2016 and that any bidders invited to participate in a second phase of bidding would receive further access to management and more extensive diligence access.

events.additional_note: Voluntary withdrawal ('no longer interested'); reason 'other internal corporate priorities'.
events.source_quote: On June 12, 2016, Strategic 2 informed representatives of Barclays that because of other internal corporate priorities, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company.

events.additional_note: Voluntary withdrawal ('no longer interested'); reason 'internal focus on other corporate transactions and a perceived overlap in technologies'. Strategic 3 had signed NDA and attended mgmt presentation but did not submit IOI by 6/9.
events.source_quote: On June 14, 2016, Strategic 3 informed representatives of Barclays that because of its internal focus on other corporate transactions and a perceived overlap in technologies, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company.

events.additional_note: Voluntary (§I1) — Sponsor A informed Barclays it would not be meaningfully higher than its 6/9 IOI and self-ceased participation. 'Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor A and Barclays or any other Company representatives.'
events.source_quote: On June 15, 2016, Sponsor A informed Barclays that in light of its view of the Company after its diligence, if it were to submit a second round bid, it would not be meaningfully higher than the price indicated in its June 9, 2016 preliminary indication of interest, and inquired whether it should continue in the strategic process. Barclays informed Sponsor A that it did not believe that the Board would be interested in a transaction at a valuation at essentially the same level as Sponsor A’s previous indication of interest, and that Barclays would inform Sponsor A if the Board determined otherwise. Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor A and Barclays or any other Company representatives.

events.process_conditions_note: Marked drafts of merger agreement requested by 7/7/2016; final bid deadline 7/8/2016.
events.additional_note: Chairperson approved final bid deadline of 7/8/2016; Barclays sent final bid process letters the same day. Only Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo remained in final round.
events.source_quote: Later on June 24, 2016, Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, requesting marked drafts of the Company’s proposed form of merger agreement (which would be provided in due course) by July 7, 2016, and setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016.

events.additional_note: Target-cut dropout — Sponsor B signaled its final bid would be 'significantly below' its 6/9 IOI; Barclays informed Sponsor B that the Special Committee would not be interested in a lower-valuation transaction, ending discussions.
events.source_quote: On June 29, 2016, Sponsor B informed Barclays that in light of its revised views on the Company’s growth outlook, if it were to submit a final bid, it would be significantly below the price indicated in its June 9, 2016 preliminary indication of interest, and inquired whether it should continue its diligence efforts. Barclays informed Sponsor B that it did not believe that the Special Committee would be interested in a transaction at a valuation significantly lower than Sponsor B’s previous indication of interest, and that Barclays would inform Sponsor B if the Special Committee determined otherwise. Following this discussion there were no further discussions between Sponsor B and Barclays or any other Company representatives.

events.process_conditions_note: Equity commitment for entire purchase price; TB indicated it had completed all due diligence and was prepared to execute its revised draft of the merger agreement.
events.source_quote: On July 8, 2016, the date by which Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had been invited to submit their final bids, only Thoma Bravo submitted a bid for the Company. Thoma Bravo’s bid was at a price of $19.00 per share in cash, provided for an equity commitment for the entire purchase price and indicated that Thoma Bravo had completed all of its due diligence and was prepared to execute its revised draft of the merger agreement and announce the transaction prior to the market open on July 11, 2016.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1 — submitted in response to final bid process letter (§K2 process-letter trigger), accompanied by revised draft merger agreement ('markup of the merger agreement' / 'definitive agreement submitted' formal triggers), completed due diligence, equity commitment for entire price.

events.additional_note: Final-round deadline; only Thoma Bravo submitted.
events.source_quote: On July 8, 2016, the date by which Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo had been invited to submit their final bids, only Thoma Bravo submitted a bid for the Company.

events.process_conditions_note: Revised equity commitment letter for entire purchase price; TB indicated $19.25 was its best and final offer.
events.source_quote: Later on July 9, 2016, Barclays received a revised written, non-binding proposal from Thoma Bravo setting forth its best and final offer of $19.25 per share, together with a revised draft of the equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price for the Company. The revised offer indicated that Thoma Bravo desired to execute the definitive merger agreement and announce the transaction prior to market open on July 11, 2016. Representatives of Barclays engaged in discussions with representatives of Thoma Bravo in order to obtain additional information regarding the terms of, and conditions to, its proposal. In those discussions, Thoma Bravo made it clear that $19.25 per share was its best and final offer.

flags.reason: Filing describes the $19.25 offer as 'revised written, non-binding proposal' yet also explicitly 'best and final offer' — the 'best and final' trigger wins per §G1 precedence; 'non-binding' label reflects legal status pre-execution. Formal classification upheld.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1 — filing explicitly calls this 'best and final offer' (formal trigger); accompanied by revised equity commitment letter for entire price; submitted during final round negotiation window.

events.process_conditions_note: Termination fee $13.6 million (~2.5% of equity value). Matching rights: 3 business days on superior proposals, +2 days on material revisions. Equity commitment letter and voting agreements executed; no debt financing required. Thoma Bravo Fund guarantee capped at $40.8 million.
events.additional_note: Merger agreement executed prior to market open 7/13; joint press release issued same morning (§prompt non-negotiable: Sale Press Release evidence folded into Executed row).
source_quote: On July 13, 2016, before the stock market opened, the parties finalized and executed the merger agreement and received executed final copies of the equity commitment letter and the voting agreements. Later on July 13, 2016, before the stock market opened, the Company and Thoma Bravo issued a joint press release announcing the execution of the merger agreement.
Imprivata has agreed to pay Parent approximately $13.6 million in cash upon the termination of the merger agreement under certain circumstances (which we refer to as the “ termination fee ”).
